item.title: This is why you're my favorite author
item.content:encoded:
I didn't think I could love dado any harder but him joking about being an "enlightened centrist" and all but saying ACAB proved that that wasn't true.

Easiest +1 of my life.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's time to stan

item.title: Re: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: No.

item.title: Re: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Is there spam forums for people who specifically want to spam, because spamming is loads of fun.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Excuse me DJ-Cactus but I am not sure that FennecFax was talking about you I am pretty sure he was talking about the guy who wrote a ton of words on it on the post that he replied to.

Good article, its pretty funny, possible GAW x dado crossover in future??

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
you literally go to a forum and just shit on the whole idea of it just to spread political commentary

On the contrary, I wrote an article about it. The mainlist isn't usually considered a forum in the traditional sense.

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: FennecFax, instead of posting multiple comments in succession, please edit your previous post using the "edit" function under the "options" tab to the lower right of every comment. That prevents spam buildup, and it's in the rules.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why is the object classified as safe despite the fact that it is uncontainable in its nature, seems like poor writing, safe is typically kept to items that are easily contained

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bro, this is just so pathetic, you literally go to a forum and just shit on the whole idea of it just to spread political commentary, and yeah, I know I didn't need to comment

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sad to see cops getting hated on so much recently, however i will keep my own politics out of this as the work was well written and should not be downvoted for simple squabbles of ideology.

The only problem I see is that recently the subjects of the articles provided by the creator have been increasingly political and more dark in nature, I support the site's ability to allow free thought on all subject matters, but the increase in politics of ANY sort is a concerning thought to me as works that use it usually only do so to either send a message or to garner support via political bias of the reader, a tactic that can quickly create splits in communities and breed hate between members.

However, I must admit the piece was clever, the politics were the opinions of dado and thus cleverly allows users of the site to make their own opinion of the matter, and is lighthearted enough to not cause either side of the spectrum to devolve into defensive screaming, but productive exchange of ideas and opinions for both parties while they laugh at the articles fun writing style. +1

item.title: This isn't a -J?
item.content:encoded:
You're one of my fave authors but I felt this was uncharacteristically blunt? At any other time it would have been less noteworthy or more comedic at least, but with the current unrest, the entire thing felt like an obvious appeal to a political agenda.

Like someone else said, maybe it's just because I'm a frontline medic and my personal involvement is giving me a pretty raw viewpoint, but I don't think I enjoyed this one as much as I was hoping to.

I love your work, I'm looking forwards to your next SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
get that nut u crave

dado x reader smut fanfic when

item.title: Finally
item.content:encoded: Ever since discovering this site 2 years ago, I've unsuccessfully tried to get my wife to enjoy them as much as I do. I should have known it would take Dado to finally pique her interest. Spousal +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are so so so many things I almost wrote here…. +1
dado is good and patriotic american who supports our brave officers

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Apparently, dado is a based radical centrist who believes in ACAB. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why does the fact that dado somehow knows that the Foundation is the interviewer crack me up so much? It's an easy joke that isn't that funny, but it somehow turned my entire opinion of the article around.

Take your +1. I don't know if you deserve it, but you're getting it anyways.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The concept as a whole was already great, and then a few bits lifted it into “actually laughed out loud for real” territory:

  • The “behave” donut for the National Guatd.
  • “dado is of enlightened centrism.”
  • “the bezos or 420 elon.”
  • dado ending the dialogue with pig emojis.
  • The phrase “four to five hundred pound hog” is somehow inately hilarious.
  • And many others, these are just the first few that leapt to mind.

Also, I’m normally not a fan of the ACS header, but this might be the first time I’ve actually liked it. Something about the over-the-top serious, formal, sci-fi look of the header followed immediately by dado’s WordArt really does it for me.

Beautiful. An easy +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: dado isnt a joke sir. dado is serius businesman.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: okay okay dado is not only a centrist but a radical centrist? wtf I love him more now
based

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Perhaps I've just been made too cynical by all the fake solidarity I've seen across the web recently. While I still don't like the article I'll retract the comment about it being performative.

As for the bail funds and other places to donate, I've been on that. It's the least a person can do as long as they've got relatively stable finances.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "dado is of enlightened centrism" my sides were in SHAMBLES after that one, +1
Agree!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the idea and its a fine article I just feel like if you were gonna make something a complete joke why not just make it a -j article? Idk maybe I'm being nitpicky. Novote for now though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm so happy to read this scp
+10101

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dunno what other response you expected. Everyone on this website is free to give whatever critique they think is warranted but I by no means have to appreciate or even respect that critique. Comparing something I've written to corporate solidarity tweets and calling it uncomfortably performative is something I reject outright.

Everyone deals with things in their own way. I am confined to a dark little corner of the world where speaking out in excess would be a quick way for me to find myself unemployed and ostracized. I am fortunate that I have this platform to express these frustrations in the only way I know how - unapologetic mockery. This is not nuanced political discussion. This is a funny story about dado making cops shit pigs because I like writing dado and also fuck cops.

Not every gesture is insincere. Not every act is performative. You can hate a thing and make jokes at that thing's expense and not have an ulterior motive. If nothing else, this should be the continuation of a trend that I have developed over many years of writing here - GWB is a wicked buffoon, Trump is a dumb egomaniac, the alt-right is an unfunny joke and cops can kindly fuck off. Not saying something in response to this, the newest vile tragedy in our sisyphean march towards annihilation, would be out of character.

You are welcome to believe whatever you want to believe, insomuch that there is nothing I can do physically or otherwise to convince you of something other than what you have decided. However, as someone who has - for better or worse - set themselves alight on this altar of disdain for these systems, please let me say clearly for everyone to hear, yourself included: please do not ascribe your own motivations to my actions as if they are gospel. I am what I am, not what you would intend me to be.

That said, if you've gotten this far and want to do something worthwhile, please check out the Community Justice Exchange's national bail fund directory. If you, like me, feel powerless to do anything other than say mean things about true jackasses on the internet, please remember that there are groups doing real good out there and those groups need money to function. I would encourage anyone to contribute, if you are able.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
'dado only want 2 grill'

Nearly broke my mouse upvoting this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ok

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes, I'll keep my ideals nice and quiet and not so much as breathe a word of it in my fiction so as to not inconvenience anyone who doesn't want to read what I, the author, believe about things.

This is an article about donuts that make cops shit pigs. This is not nuanced commentary - it is humor.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
between this, 4444, and 5004, the spirit of A Modest Proposal lives on imo

+1312

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The only problem I have with this article is that the poor hogs have to suffer when they're shat out. Otherwise great and a solid +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's difficult to pull apart how honestly this SCP should be taken but I'm going to take it at "totally fiction inspired by real world" value and say that this is ok. +1. I think dado's chat with the foundation was funny enough for me to up vote. Everything else, I don't feel enough to change my vote.

And, yes, I am evaluating this on its comedic appeal. I know the real world is horrific but I feel that this doesn't cross the line and remains free enough of that horror to be by itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I dunno. I found this obnoxiously hamfisted, and uncomfortably performative. I don't think writing up a dado pig-shitting donut restaurant is the most respectful way to vent given the tragedy we're still reeling from, and I'm not talking about respect towards cops. If I'm going to be honest the article comes off as having as much sincerity as corporate solidarity tweets.

Fuck it, I'm going to have to downvote. I can't bring myself to like this or agree with the way its trying to push its message.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't know man, you used to be one of my favorite authors on the site, with your politics being very subtle and organic in your stories, but now it's just too damn on the nose and blatant.

Fun fact: Subtlety is for fools and cowards!

In more serious terms, there's nothing inherently virtuous or good about being subtle, and it's sometimes more appropriate to simply say the things you mean out right, so they don't get ignored by those without any ability to look deeply into a work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't know man, you used to be one of my favorite authors on the site, with your politics being very subtle and organic in your stories, but now it's just too damn on the nose and blatant.
Left or right, I don't care what the politics are, but it really takes me out of the immersion when it devolves into basically reading your political rants, instead of one of your older, more subtle stories where your political ideals weren't so apparent.
Also, this is like, the third "dude cops are pigs" article I've read on this sight, and it's just simply getting boring.
That explains my down vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll never understand why people think comments like these are a good idea. You could have easily communicated what you intended to communicate in a way that wasn't so easily dismissed, and I wish you would have.

item.title: Mod Post - Closed
item.content:encoded:
Don't use slurs in the forums, and try to be less dickish in your comments. It's against the site rules.

This is a closed staff post. Do not respond unless you are staff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ah yes, relating your political opinions to a morally grey [snip] who tries to help but frequently just causes unnecessary havoc while not actually solving the problem he was hired to solve.

It's like pottery.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gonna have to downvote this one, for the same reason I downvoted SCP-4670. I think the idea is funny, but this is incredibly crude and ham-fisted, especially with the overt references to internet memes ("enlightened centrist", "I just wanna grill"). I'm not saying that articles can't have a political message (SCP-3003 pulls that off very well). It just shouldn't be one of this nature.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: dado is my buddy

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Staff Notice: this user has been permanently banned for trolling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
pretty much made an account just to upvote this, while the whole thing is great, the national guard donut was a really good touch

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nah this one is pretty good, thanks though!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I see… guess it’s just a bad one. Happens to all of us

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
now you've got me wanting a donut

but not like… not in a pig-shitting way… just… take the upvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed. It’s in poor taste. Good stuff but bad timing for sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At any other time, I probably would have upvoted this in a heartbeat. But I’ve spent the last two weeks as a medic, getting beaten and shot at, and anything that puts this in such a joking and jovial way just feels off-putting and patronizing. People are dying out there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: no, it's definitely dado.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
as if dado would care about police brutality of all things.

this is GAW false flagging dado. can't convince me otherwise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: cool

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Despite the many terrible things I am having to through this week, this managed to make me cackle with laughter. Thank you.

I particularly enjoy the "u have nice day foundationman" at the end.

Everything about this is hilarious. I have been sharing it with abandon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Runic pretty much summarized it all. A pretty consensual (and, currently very popular) political stance in a SCP who looks more like an immature bashing than something really worthwhile, be it by the thoughts it provokes, the angle it chooses…
A bit like a child's drawing in which a teacher he doesn't like is being drowned in poop jokes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My distaste for dado aside, this is just bad political satire. Instead of making clever observations about the specifics of the issue, it's just mindless slapstick aimed at a body of power that the creator doesn't like, exploiting their current extreme unpopularity (especially within the circle the work's been posted in) to appeal to the crowd as a substitute for real thought-provoking content.

I support BLM, but I don't support "bad guy wrestler from USSR gets beat up by Hulk Hogan"-level commentary. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1: dah-doh pronunciation is finally canon.

2: this is extremely hilarious.

3: "the act of passing a four to five hundred pound hog" is not metrics, especially becaus you use kilograms in the next paragraph.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How this animal, which is otherwise non-anomalous, manifests within the subject's bowels is currently poorly understood.

+1

acab what is dado said yes

Absolutely brilliant

item.title: first dado article i've upvoted
item.content:encoded: many genuine laughs enjoyed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
based and dadopilled
+1

item.title: Re: This is nuts.
item.content:encoded: Sadly, in what is an example of a peak reddit momento, it was not. This is why I stopped using that site for anything other than SCP stuff.
